title: Context Templates
description: Understand template types and their benefits. Create product context templates, document templates, and CAD document templates to standardize configuration.

topics.title: Windchill Templates Overview

content.text: Object templates are a foundational mechanism in Windchill that drive many day-to-day activities. Whether a user is creating a document, starting a new product, defining a workflow, or establishing a life cycle, templates provide the starting point. Each template type offers a unique set of features, and users only see and interact with the templates that administrators have enabled for their context.
User-Facing Template Types

rows: Provide desired style, structure, and layout for documentation
A contract template with a corporate logo, boilerplate legal text, and standard formatting
Provide initial settings for CAD authoring files
A Creo part template with predefined units, material defaults, and layer settings
Provide standard boilerplate text for notes used to mark up designs or structures
A design review note template with required comment fields and approval sections

content.text: Templates deliver two categories of benefit. For users, they provide consistent style, structure, layout, and standard starting points that reduce repetitive work. For administrators, they enforce corporate standards and provide a single point to update and roll out changes across the organization.
Template Access and Visibility

Template availability depends on where the template is created and the user's context. Project, program, product, and library templates are restricted to their respective creators and context participants. Document templates, by contrast, are available to a wider group of users because they serve general authoring needs across multiple contexts.

content.prompt: Match each template type to the benefit it provides:

pairs.right: Consistent style, structure, and layout for documentation
Predefined initial settings for CAD authoring files
Standard boilerplate text for marking up designs and structures

keyTakeaways: Object templates drive key Windchill activities including document creation, product setup, workflow definition, and life cycle establishment
The three user-facing template types are Document Templates, CAD Document Templates, and Note Templates
Templates benefit users with consistent starting points and benefit administrators with centralized standards enforcement
Template visibility depends on where it was created; document templates are broadly available while context templates are restricted to their creators

topics.title: Context Templates

content.text: Context templates define the initial business environment settings that are applied when creating new products, libraries, organizations, projects, or programs. Unlike user-facing templates that help with individual documents or CAD files, context templates set up the entire administrative framework for a new context. Only organization administrators have access to create and manage context templates.
Context Template Types

rows: General Library, Part Library, Document Library

content.text: What Context Templates Establish

cards.back: Context structure (folders and domains), default forum topics, reference notebook folders, and user notebook folders are all defined by the template.

Context participation settings including roles, teams, and groups are established. Access control policies are written to context team roles - this is the most significant administrative element of a context template.
Data types, object templates, and business rules available within the context are configured. Default life cycle and workflow templates are also set.
For project and program contexts, templates can include project plans and deliverables that provide a structured starting point for project execution.

content.text: The most significant administrative element in a context template is the series of access control rules written to context team roles. These rules determine who can view, create, modify, and delete objects in the new context. Getting these rules right in the template prevents security and access issues in every context created from it.
Benefits of Context Templates

Context templates ease the administrative burden of creating new contexts by providing a complete, pre-configured starting point. They also simplify migration and upgrade processes because the template captures the full business environment configuration. Administrators can use templates to transfer proven business environments between Windchill installations, ensuring consistency across departments or sites.

content.prompt: Arrange these context template benefits from most immediate to most long-term impact:
items: Ease the administrative burden of new context creation
Enforce consistent business environment settings across contexts
Transfer business environments between Windchill installations

keyTakeaways: Context templates define the initial business environment for new products, libraries, organizations, projects, and programs
Only organization administrators can create and manage context templates
The most significant element in a context template is the access control rules written to context team roles
Context templates ease creation, migration, and upgrade burdens while enabling business environment transfer between installations

topics.title: Document and CAD Templates

content.text: Document and CAD document templates provide standardized starting points for content creation. While they serve similar goals of consistency and efficiency, they differ in how they are created, scoped, and managed. Understanding these differences helps administrators deploy the right templates at the right organizational level.
CAD Document Templates

rows: Individual part models with predefined parameters and settings
Assembly structures with predefined component relationships
Engineering drawings with standard borders, title blocks, and views
Schematic and wiring diagrams with standard symbols and layouts
Manufacturing process models and toolpath definitions
Additional CAD document types not covered by the standard categories

content.text: CAD Template Scoping

CAD document templates follow a hierarchical scoping model that controls their availability. A template created at the site level is available to all organizations and their sub-contexts. A template created at the organization level is available to all contexts within that organization. A template created at a sub-organization level is available only within that specific context. This scoping hierarchy allows administrators to provide enterprise-wide standards while permitting local customization where needed.

CAD Part and Assembly templates are particularly useful for a top-down design approach because they allow users to create a WTPart and its associated CAD document in a single action, linking the product structure and design data from the start.
Document Templates

Document templates are available by document type and provide a content file as the starting point for new documents. They can be created at the organization level or at the context level. Unlike CAD templates, document templates do not require an external authoring application to create the initial template file - any supported content file can serve as the template.

Templates maintain an iteration history that tracks changes related to upgrades. This means administrators can update a template over time and retain a record of how it has evolved, which is valuable during system upgrades and audits.

content.prompt: Arrange these CAD template scoping levels from broadest to narrowest availability:
items: Site level - available to all organizations and sub-contexts
Organization level - available to all contexts in that organization
Sub-organization level - available only within that specific context

keyTakeaways: CAD document templates come in six types: Part, Assembly, Drawing, Diagram, Manufacturing, and Other
CAD template scoping follows a hierarchy: site level (broadest), organization level, sub-organization level (narrowest)
CAD Part and Assembly templates support top-down design by creating a WTPart and CAD document in a single action
Document templates provide content files as starting points and track iteration history related to upgrades

topics.title: Exercise 4 - Create a Product Context Template

content.text: PTC Publications needs to ensure that every new product context starts with the same configuration. In this exercise, you will create a product context template from an existing product and then verify it works by creating a new product from that template.
You will sign in as bpatterson/ptc for this exercise. Make sure the PTC New Publication Template product exists before proceeding.

content.title: Create a Product Context Template
content.objective: Save an existing product as a template and create a new product from that template to verify it works.

steps.action: Sign in to Windchill as bpatterson with the password ptc.
steps.detail: This user has the necessary organization administrator privileges to create and manage context templates.

steps.action: Navigate to PTC New Publication Template and open the Details page.
steps.detail: You will save this existing product as a template so it can be reused for future product contexts.
steps.hint: Use the Quick Links or Recent Products to find the PTC New Publication Template product.

steps.action: Select Actions > Save as Template.
steps.detail: The Save as Template action captures the product's configuration - including folder structure, roles, access control policies, and business rules - into a reusable template.

steps.action: Set the Template Name to PTC New Publication and add the description: Template for the new PTC Publications.
steps.detail: Remove the duplicate word 'Template' from the default name. A clear, concise name helps other administrators identify the template's purpose.
steps.hint: The default name may include 'Template' twice - remove the extra occurrence.

steps.action: Clear the Team Members checkbox and click OK.
steps.detail: Clearing the Team Members checkbox prevents specific user assignments from being copied into the template. The template will retain the roles and groups but not individual user memberships.

steps.action: Navigate to PTC Publications > Templates > Product Templates.
steps.detail: This is where all product templates for the PTC Publications organization are listed.

steps.action: Verify that the PTC New Publication Template is listed.
steps.detail: Confirming the template appears in the Product Templates list ensures it was saved correctly and is available for use.
steps.hint: If the template does not appear, check that you saved it from the correct product and refresh the page.

tasks.title: Create a Product Using the Template

steps.action: Navigate to Recent Products > View All > New Product.
steps.detail: This begins the process of creating a new product context that will inherit its configuration from the template.

steps.action: Set the Name to Greek Cookbook and select PTC New Publication Template as the Template.
steps.detail: Selecting the template ensures the new product inherits the folder structure, access control policies, and other configuration from the original product.
steps.hint: Use the Template dropdown or browse to find PTC New Publication Template.

steps.action: Click OK to create the product.
steps.detail: Windchill creates the new product context and applies all settings from the selected template.

steps.action: Navigate to Greek Cookbook > Folder Contents and verify that the Team Information folder already exists.
steps.detail: The presence of the Team Information folder confirms that the folder structure was inherited from the template. This demonstrates how templates standardize new context creation.
steps.hint: If the folder does not appear, verify the correct template was selected during product creation.

keyTakeaways: Product context templates are created using Actions > Save as Template from an existing product
Clearing the Team Members checkbox prevents individual user assignments from being copied into the template while retaining roles and groups
New products created from a template inherit the folder structure, access control policies, and other configuration settings
Templates are managed under the organization's Templates > Product Templates section

topics.title: Exercise 5 - Create a Document Template

content.text: Technical writers at PTC Publications need to use the same document format that includes mandatory information such as a description, bibliography, and standard layout. In this exercise, you will create a document template and then test it by creating a new document from the template.
The content file UnitTemplate.docx should be available on your system for upload. This file contains the standard table of contents and index layout required by PTC Publications.

content.title: Create a Document Template
content.objective: Create a document template from an uploaded content file and verify it works by creating a new document from the template.

steps.action: Navigate to PTC Publications > Templates > Document Templates.
steps.detail: Document templates are managed at the organization or context level. This location stores all document templates available to contexts within PTC Publications.

steps.action: Click to create a new document template.
steps.detail: A new document template requires a type, a content file, and descriptive metadata.
steps.hint: Look for a 'New Document Template' or similar action in the Document Templates area.

steps.action: Set the Type to Outline and browse to upload UnitTemplate.docx as the content file.
steps.detail: The Outline type is appropriate for structured documents that include a table of contents, sections, and index. The uploaded file becomes the starting point for all documents created from this template.
steps.hint: Use the Browse button to locate UnitTemplate.docx on your local system.

steps.action: Set the Name to Unit Template and the Description to Standard Template with TOC and Index.
steps.detail: A descriptive name and description help users identify the right template when creating new documents.

steps.action: Click Finish to save the document template.
steps.detail: The template is now available for use when creating new Outline-type documents within this organization.

steps.action: Navigate to PTC New Publication Template and create a new document.
steps.detail: You will test the template by creating a document within an existing product context.
steps.hint: Look for a Create New Document action in the product context.

steps.action: Set the Type to Outline, select Unit Template as the template, and set Primary Content Source to No Content.
steps.detail: Selecting 'No Content' for the primary content source means the document will use the template's content file as its starting point rather than uploading a new file.

steps.action: Set the Name to Doc Template Test and click OK.
steps.detail: The document is created and checked out to you automatically, allowing immediate editing.

steps.action: Download and view the file to verify that the template content (TOC, index, standard layout) is present.
steps.detail: Confirming the template content appears in the new document verifies that the template is working correctly. The document should contain the table of contents and index structure from UnitTemplate.docx.
steps.hint: Right-click the document or use the Download action to save the file locally and open it.

keyTakeaways: Document templates are created by specifying a type, uploading a content file, and providing descriptive metadata
The uploaded content file becomes the starting point for all documents created from the template
Documents created from a template are automatically checked out to the creator for immediate editing
Testing a template by creating a document from it verifies that the content and structure are applied correctly
